reason: The statement applies because the text describes a situation where Nebuchadnezzar, the king of Babylon, commands all people to worship a golden image he set up and threatens with punishment in a fiery furnace for those who do not comply. Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, who are Jews, refuse to worship the golden image, citing their loyalty to their own God. This refusal to worship a foreign idol aligns with warning the Israelites or Sons of Israel against idolatry and disobeying the commandments of their God.
